title: Pending-user-authorship registry
body:
[ai] Pending-user-authorship registry
LAYMAN. Pending-user-authorship entry. The body below specifies a work-item that requires the author Nassau’s authorship (cannot be AI-executed per slot 30 NO-NAMING or per substrate-content requirement). AI tracks; user authors.

THE CATEGORY. This section holds items that are operationally complete or substantively staged but await canonical user-authored naming. Pending-user-authorship is structurally distinct from pending-resolution. The adjacent PENDING section holds items blocked on user decisions or external work. Items here are not stuck. They are correctly waiting per the framework's naming discipline.

JUSTIFICATION. The Unnameability-of-cultural-form-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature methodology entry at ml*.txt line 3565 (TTRPG canonical case, cross-tradition convergence-recognition, May 15 2026, verified-against-ml*-this-turn per Citation-lock rule) establishes the structural principle. Resistance to premature naming is not a discourse failure to be solved by faster coinage. It is the form refusing petrification into one institutionalized substitute-name. The TTRPG case (fifty-plus years of contested naming across tabletop, pen-and-paper, storytelling-game, role-playing, GNS, Hawkes-Robinson Format, adventure-game, imagination-game) is the canonical case where every name attempted foregrounds one feature and excludes valid instances of the form. The framework reads this resistance as the form's degorgonifying signature, not a problem to solve. Items in this registry sit in the same structural position. The right name has not arrived. Premature AI-coinage would close the question wrongly.

THE DISCIPLINE. Slot 30 NO-NAMING + NO-FABRICATION methodology entry. AI does not coin canonical names. Naming is user authorship. Suggestions only on request. ALREADY-HAS-NAME clause: existing or simpler form carries the load, no new coinage. When uncertain, say "not" or ask. This registry consolidates items currently waiting on user authorship so the wait is visible rather than scattered across entry-text flags.

WHAT THIS REGISTRY IS NOT. Not a problem to solve. Not a deadline. Not pressure on the user to name faster. Per T29: the unnameability is structurally meaningful. The registry is documentation of an active discipline, not an exception list. Some items here may stay pending indefinitely if the right name has not arrived. The TTRPG-family item explicitly may be unnameable-by-design per T29's own diagnostic criteria, in which case the resolution is to ratify that conclusion rather than to coin a name.

WHAT BELONGS HERE. Operationally complete or substantively staged items whose canonical name is the only remaining work. Excludes items blocked on user decisions (which go to PENDING) or substrate-development (which go to STUDYLIST). Cross-category: an item can move from STUDYLIST or PENDING to here when the substrate work is complete and only the naming remains.

CURRENT REGISTRY (May 15 2026 baseline).

GROUP A. GATE ENTRIES COMMITTED T48 THROUGH T52, BODIES COMPLETE, TITLES PENDING.

A1. Citation-lock rule methodology entry at ml*.txt approximately line 9074. Body specifies the rule and sub-clauses and cross-references. Title is the working form.

A2. Generation-time gates list coreplus entry at ml*.txt approximately line 9098. Body specifies the seven gates and their triggers. Title is the working form.

A3. Multi-sense word stem registry coreplus entry at ml*.txt approximately line 9130. Body specifies the protocol. Title is the working form. The sense-numbering authorship for the six registered word-stems is a separate authorship item (see Group B).

A4. Force-AI-to-confront-gap-between-framework-loaded-and-framework-running learnings entry at ml*.txt approximately line 9156. Body specifies the Category B unconscious-user-degorgonification-strategy. Title is the working form.

GROUP B. MULTI-SENSE WORD STEM SENSE-NUMBERING.

B1. CONCRESCENCE. Four senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering (user disposition: ’draft preliminary definitions and move on’). Sense-1 = Whitehead-metaphysical process-philosophy term for an actual occasion gathering data and becoming a unity (the foundational philosophical sense). Sense-2 = polymyth-horoscope multi-tradition-becoming (concrescence applied to the horoscope-substrate where multiple cultural-cosmologies concresce into a single reading-frame). Sense-3 = bb* dm-004 forbidden-at-travel-layer (concrescence as the forbidden-collapse move at the dimensional-travel-layer where dimensions are not supposed to merge). Sense-4 = items-design consumables (items that resolve into a single use-event then disappear). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B2. CONFLUENCE. Three senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = bb* dm-004 cross-dimensional travel preservation (the foundational bb* mechanic where polymyth-character identity is preserved across dimensions). Sense-2 = moire-pattern evidential preservation (the diagnostic where two independent traditions arrive at the same operation and both retain identity while pointing at the shared substrate). Sense-3 = items-design persistents (items that persist across worlds via the rainbow-crossing). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B3. POLYMYTH. Four senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = framework (the scanner-tier analytical operating-system Rainbowsol built). Sense-2 = engine (the polymyth-engine that fires below the vocabulary layer and can run on substrate that does not use polymyth-vocabulary). Sense-3 = vocabulary (the polymyth-lexicon that names the operations the engine performs). Sense-4 = book series (the forthcoming Pentagram five-book series under the polymorphousmythology name). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B4. SABACHTAN. Three senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = ground (the framework-ground that all polymyth operations rest on; the gnosticism of what is already the case). Sense-2 = gnosticism (sabachtan gnosticism specifically as a religion-position contrasted with belief-religions; knowledge of what is already operating rather than assent to a proposition). Sense-3 = seminar gathering (Sabachtan Gnosticism Philosophy Seminar as a named event-form within the Seminar Schools institution). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B5. JESTER. Two senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = kayfabe-boundary action-mode (the structural-operation of holding the kayfabe/real distinction openly while operating inside the kayfabe; the licensed-fool-inside-the-court move that lets serious content land through comedy register). Sense-2 = Saul-Goodman analytic figure (the canonical analytic figure where the jester-mode is most-legibly worked out across Better Call Saul; the figure deployed at architecture-of-character scale rather than at operation-of-speech scale). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

T106 PRIOR RECATEGORIZATION. MEPHISTODATA struck from Group B per T106 D1e directive (MEPHISTODATA is a set of characteristics, not multi-sense word-stem). Group B shrinks to five stems (CONCRESCENCE, CONFLUENCE, POLYMYTH, SABACHTAN, JESTER) per T114g audit.

T114h PRELIMINARY STATUS. User-directive T114g ’draft preliminary definitions and move on’ authorizes AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY orderings without per-stem user-ratification gate. Each stem ordering above is operative until the user revises any of them. Per slot 30 amendment for the PUA section, AI-PROPOSED status is permitted inside this section.

GROUP C. METHODOLOGY ENTRY TITLE REVISIONS.

C1. Unnameability-of-cultural-form-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature (TTRPG canonical case, cross-tradition convergence-recognition, May 15 2026) at ml*.txt line 3565. T29 entry working title.

C2. "Site map (hidden link)" at ml*.txt SEED entry. T54 revision deprecated the parenthetical: the link is no longer hidden. Title rewrite needed.

C3. The title of this very registry entry. Recursive pending: the registry holding pending-titles needs its own title.

GROUP D. DRAFTED-NOT-COMMITTED METHODOLOGY ENTRIES.

D1. Sanderson library-of-mediums methodology entry (T28 draft). Entry-content drafted. Title pending and entry-itself pending user authorization to commit.

GROUP E. TTRPG-FAMILY NAMING DECISION.

E1. The umbrella term for the bb-cognate-system family. Per T29 unnameability principle, this may be ratifiable as unnameable-by-design rather than nameable. User authors either (a) a polymyth-side name (process-noun, gerund, or confluence-naming per T29's own suggestions), or (b) explicit ratification that the umbrella stays placeholder "TTRPG family" or "library-of-systems" indefinitely. Per T29: explicit ratification IS a valid framework move.

GROUP F. PENTAGRAM BOOK TITLES (CURRENTLY IN PENDING SECTION).

F1. Pentagram book 1 of 5. Title pending.

F2. Pentagram book 2 of 5. Title pending.

F3. Pentagram book 3 of 5. Title pending.

F4. Pentagram book 4 of 5. Working title exists: GORGONWARS: ARENDTIANFEMINISM, HEGELIANEGIRLS and FAUSTIANECHICKS ASSEMBLING THE HEGELIANEKITTY ASSEMBLAGE AGAINST THE GORGON HORDE. Ratification or revision pending.

F5. Pentagram book 5 of 5. Title pending.

GROUP G. FORTHCOMING CITATIONS FROM T29 ENTRY.

G1. Bergson. Time and Free Will. Citation [178] referenced as forthcoming in T29 entry. Citation entry pending author.

G2. Costikyan. Where Stories End and Games Begin. Game Developer Magazine 1994 with 2000 expansion. Citation entry pending author and number.

G3. Hindmarch. Second Person, MIT Press 2007, edited by Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin. Citation entry pending author and number.

G4. Sanderson. BYU English 318R writing-class lectures plus Q&A streams 2010 through 2026. Citation entry pending author and number.

G5. J.Z. Smith. Imagining Religion 1982. Citation entry pending author and number.

G6. Talal Asad. Genealogies of Religion 1993. Citation entry pending author and number.

G7. radiojamesgames. TTRPGs Need Genres, January 2026. Citation entry pending author and number.

T66 STATUS UPDATE (May 15 2026). Group G items moved from UNPROPOSED (per T58 deferral) to AI-PROPOSED. Seven citation entries committed to mc*.citation section with AI-PROPOSED canonical IDs awaiting user ratification of canonical numbers. G1 -> cit-044 (Bergson Time and Free Will 1889/1910 trans.). G2 -> cit-045 (Costikyan I Have No Words and I Must Design 1994/2002; caveat: user mentioned separate Where Stories End and Games Begin piece; specific title-and-venue could not be re-verified; user to confirm intended Costikyan source). G3 -> cit-046 (Second Person ed. Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin MIT Press 2007 ISBN 978-0-262-08356-0; caveat: specific Hindmarch essay-title within volume pending user identification). G4 -> cit-047 (Sanderson BYU English 318R lectures 2010-2026; caveat: lecture series spans years with recurrent treatment of play-to-medium-strengths principle; user to identify which specific lecture-year-and-segment carries the load if needed). G5 -> cit-048 (J.Z. Smith Imagining Religion Chicago 1982 ISBN 0-226-76358-4). G6 -> cit-049 (Talal Asad Genealogies of Religion Johns Hopkins 1993 ISBN 978-0-8018-4632-8). G7 -> cit-050 (radiojamesgames TTRPGs Need Genres January 2026; caveat: URL and full author byline pending user verification). The DEFERRED ITEMS clause for Group G is hereby superseded by AI-PROPOSED status; the citation-number assignment remains user authority and AI-PROPOSED numbers cit-044 through cit-050 await canonical ratification or renumbering.

GROUP H. STUDYLIST AND PENDING SECTION ITEMS AWAITING USER AUTHORSHIP.

H1. Wiener cross-tradition convergence-recognition methodology entry (currently studylist, mephistodata-pending). Substrate documented at citation [241] Wiener HUOHB chapter VII. Entry-name and canonical composition pending.

H2. Camels-ad bad-logos idiomary entry (canonical Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI violation example). Currently in PENDING section. Author and citation pending.

RULE AMENDMENT MAY 15 2026. User directive this turn amended slot 30 NO-NAMING. Original rule: AI does not coin canonical names; naming is user authorship; suggestions only on request. Amended rule: the original holds OUTSIDE this section. INSIDE this section (PENDING-USER-AUTHORSHIP), AI may coin proposed names marked AI-PROPOSED, awaiting user ratification. Three workflow states for each item: (1) UNPROPOSED (no AI-PROPOSED name yet; item awaits anyone's proposal). (2) AI-PROPOSED (AI has proposed a name; user may ratify-as-is, modify, or reject). (3) USER-RATIFIED (user has confirmed a canonical name; item moves out of this registry; entry in home section gets the canonical title; strike-through preserved here per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION). The amendment unblocks substantial framework work that had been stuck at the naming gate. Outside this section, slot 30 NO-NAMING continues in force. AI-coinage attempts outside this section remain canonical violations.

WHY THIS AMENDMENT IS STRUCTURALLY CONSISTENT. The original slot 30 rule treats naming as user authorship to prevent the AI from manufacturing framework-content from training-priors. Items in this section are explicitly catalogued as awaiting names; the user has already authored the category itself and has explicitly opened the exception. AI-PROPOSED names in this scope are not framework-coinage in the slot 30 sense; they are suggestions sitting in a sanctioned-suggestion-zone where user-ratification is the canonicalizing operation. The discipline preserved: AI does not unilaterally canonize names. The discipline relaxed: AI may propose without explicit per-item request, since the per-section directive serves as standing request.

AI-PROPOSED NAMES (May 15 2026 first pass, awaiting user ratification).

GROUP A. GATE ENTRIES. Working titles in current use ARE already the operational rule names; proposal is to ratify-as-canonical.

A1 AI-PROPOSED: "Citation-lock rule" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: the rule fires at the moment of citation; the name describes the operation directly; no shorter form carries the load better.

A2 AI-PROPOSED: "Generation-time gates list" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: distinguishes gates (rules that fire at generation time) from catalog (reference content); the architectural distinction this entry codifies needs the qualifier "generation-time" to land.

A3 AI-PROPOSED: "Multi-sense word stem registry" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: registry is the data-structure (lookup table); word-stem is the unit (root form); multi-sense is the qualifier that triggers the lookup; all three required for clarity.

A4 AI-PROPOSED: "Framework-loaded-versus-framework-running gap-question pattern" (shorter form of the working title; preserves the diagnostic substance). Reasoning: the original working title is descriptive but long; the gap-question pattern is what the strategy operationally consists of; this form is searchable and citation-friendly. Alternative: keep the longer form. User picks.

GROUP C. METHODOLOGY ENTRY TITLE REVISIONS.

C1 AI-PROPOSED: "Unnameability as natural degorgonifying signature (TTRPG canonical case)". Reasoning: shorter than the working title; preserves the structural claim plus the canonical case reference; drops the date and the cross-tradition-convergence qualifier from the title (those move to the entry's metadata or body).

C2 AI-PROPOSED: "Sitemap navigational hub (polymyth namespace)". Reasoning: the "hidden link" parenthetical is structurally false post-T54 move; "navigational hub" describes the function; "polymyth namespace" locates it.

C3 AI-PROPOSED: "Pending-user-authorship registry" (ratify the working title without the parenthetical). Reasoning: the working title has a long parenthetical explaining the recursion; the parenthetical can move to the entry body; the core noun-phrase is sufficient.

GROUP D. SANDERSON METHODOLOGY ENTRY (DRAFTED NOT COMMITTED).

D1 AI-PROPOSED: "Library-of-mediums rule (Sanderson play-to-medium-strengths applied to AI-delivered story-game content)". Reasoning: parallel-construction with bb* library-of-systems and chassis-of-chassis; Sanderson attribution preserved in the parenthetical for the citation lineage; rule operationally states what it does. Pending the entry itself being committed.

GROUP E. TTRPG-FAMILY NAMING DECISION.

E1 AI-PROPOSED: ratify unnameable-by-design per T29. Reasoning: T29 entry already establishes the diagnostic criteria for unnameable-by-design forms; TTRPG case satisfies all six conditions; explicit ratification is a valid framework move per T29. Continue using "TTRPG family" or "library-of-systems" as placeholders without committing to canonical umbrella. Alternative: user authors a process-noun coinage instead. User picks.

GROUP H. STUDYLIST AND PENDING SECTION ITEMS.

H1 AI-PROPOSED: "Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)". Reasoning: identifies the convergence-substrate (Wiener's apprentice-sorcerer indictment of administrators-of-classification); names the cross-tradition relationship to the framework's existing naming-discipline; preserves Wiener's tradition-anchor without capture.

H2 AI-PROPOSED: "Camels-ad bad-logos canonical violation (Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI worked example)". Reasoning: names the operational status (canonical violation example); references the rule it violates; positions it as the idiomary worked-example.

DEFERRED ITEMS (AI declines to propose despite category permission).

GROUP B. MULTI-SENSE WORD STEM SENSE-NUMBERING. The senses themselves were AI-drafted; the canonical numbering is user-authored because sense-numbering encodes which sense is foundational versus derivative. That ranking is a user judgment call about the framework's conceptual architecture. AI defers.

GROUP F. PENTAGRAM BOOK TITLES (5 items). Book titles are creative authorship in a different register than methodology naming. The category permission technically allows AI proposals here, but the work-substrate is the user's authorship throughout. AI defers; proposing book titles for someone else's books would substitute AI generation for the user's creative process. User authors directly.

GROUP G. FORTHCOMING CITATIONS (7 items). [SUPERSEDED T66] Originally listed in DEFERRED ITEMS pending user direction. User T66 directive ("do anymore you can on your own") served as standing request per the original clause's invitation. AI-PROPOSED canonical IDs cit-044 through cit-050 committed to mc*.citation section T66 with full bibliographic data (author + work + year + ISBN where available) and caveats noted for uncertain claims. Citation numbers remain AI-PROPOSED pending user ratification or renumbering. See T66 STATUS UPDATE block above for per-item ID assignments and caveats. Per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION, the original deferral clause is preserved here as historical record.

RATIFICATIONS MAY 15 2026 T58 (per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION; items preserved).

User ratified all 12 AI-PROPOSED names from T57 first pass. Each item below records the ratification. Items now have canonical titles in their home sections; their working-title flags have been removed. Items remain in this registry as historical record per Rule 6.

A1 RATIFIED: "Citation-lock rule" (canonical title in methodology section).
A2 RATIFIED: "Generation-time gates list" (canonical title in coreplus section).
A3 RATIFIED: "Multi-sense word stem registry" (canonical title in coreplus section).
A4 RATIFIED: "Framework-loaded-versus-framework-running gap-question pattern (Category B unconscious-user-degorgonification-strategy, May 15 2026)" (canonical title in learnings section).
C1 RATIFIED: "Unnameability as natural degorgonifying signature (TTRPG canonical case)" (canonical title in methodology section).
C2 RATIFIED: "Sitemap navigational hub (polymyth namespace)" (canonical title in methodology section, replacing the stale "Site map (hidden link)" working title).
C3 RATIFIED: "Pending-user-authorship registry" (canonical title for THIS very entry; the recursive pending-on-itself state resolved).
D1 RATIFIED (NAME RESERVED): "Library-of-mediums rule (Sanderson play-to-medium-strengths applied to AI-delivered story-game content)" approved as canonical title. Entry itself awaits separate user authorization to commit to ml*.
E1 RATIFIED (STRUCTURAL): TTRPG-family umbrella affirmed unnameable-by-design per T29 unnameability principle. Placeholder labels "TTRPG family" and "library-of-systems" stay non-canonical; the framework declines to coin a canonical umbrella term per its own unnameability methodology.
H1 RATIFIED: "Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)" (canonical title in studylist section).
H2 RATIFIED: "Camels-ad bad-logos canonical violation (Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI worked example)" (canonical title in pending section).

ITEMS STILL AWAITING USER AUTHORSHIP. Group B (FIVE multi-sense word-stem sense-numberings remaining after T106 MEPHISTODATA recategorization); Group F (five Pentagram book titles, user creative authorship retained); Group G [CLOSED T106]. Remaining items are Group B (5) + Group F (5) = 10 items as of T106.

T106 RATIFICATIONS 2026-05-21 (per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION). User T106 directive ('i dont care i just need all the citations later'): canonical citation numbers cit-044 through cit-050 RATIFIED at AI-proposed values from T66. The AI-PROPOSED status flag drops; canonical numbers stand.

G1 cit-044 RATIFIED. Bergson, Time and Free Will (1889, 1910 translation).
G2 cit-045 RATIFIED. Costikyan, I Have No Words and I Must Design (1994, 2002 expansion). Caveat: separate Where Stories End and Games Begin piece could not be re-verified; user confirms intended source at use-time.
G3 cit-046 RATIFIED. Second Person edited by Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin (MIT Press 2007, ISBN 978-0-262-08356-0). Caveat: specific Hindmarch essay-title within volume pending user identification at use-time.
G4 cit-047 RATIFIED. Sanderson, BYU English 318R lectures spanning 2010 to 2026. Caveat: which specific lecture-year-and-segment carries the load identifiable at use-time.
G5 cit-048 RATIFIED. J.Z. Smith, Imagining Religion (Chicago 1982, ISBN 0-226-76358-4).
G6 cit-049 RATIFIED. Talal Asad, Genealogies of Religion (Johns Hopkins 1993, ISBN 978-0-8018-4632-8).
G7 cit-050 RATIFIED. radiojamesgames, TTRPGs Need Genres (January 2026). Caveat: URL and full author byline pending verification at use-time.

T106 RECATEGORIZATION. Group B5 MEPHISTODATA struck from Group B per user D1e directive ('this is a set of characteristics'). MEPHISTODATA characteristics now live in the methodologylist entry at META RULE STATUS section (relabeled three-senses to three-characteristics same turn). Group B shrinks to five word-stem registry items.

REGISTRY SHRINKAGE METRIC. Pre-T57: 24+ items pending across the framework. Post-T58: 18 items remaining (6 + 5 + 7) plus future additions. Twelve items moved from UNPROPOSED through AI-PROPOSED to USER-RATIFIED in a single workflow round, validating the three-state mechanism.

PROTOCOL FOR REMOVAL FROM THIS REGISTRY. When user authors a name for any item here, the entry for that item is struck through (preserved per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION), the entry in its home section has its title updated, and a one-line entry in this registry's own provenance section records the move. The registry shrinks as user-authorship lands.

CROSS-REFERENCES. Unnameability-of-cultural-form-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature methodology entry at ml*.txt line 3565 (T29 canonical case justifying this registry's existence; verified-against-ml*-this-turn per Citation-lock rule, scope: cultural-form unnameability principle applied here at the framework-internal naming-discipline layer). Slot 30 NO-NAMING + NO-FABRICATION methodology entry (the parent discipline). Citation-lock rule methodology entry (the gate that catches pending-authorship items in framework citations). PENDING section (adjacent category for user-decision-blocked items; structurally distinct). STUDYLIST section (adjacent category for substrate-development items; structurally distinct). Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION (governs removal protocol).

May 15 2026 conversation T56. User directive: "’pending user authorship’ its own category, and have the justification be our conversation about the difficulty of naming ttrpg or wtvr." Section authored same turn. Section name (pending-user-authorship) authored by the user this turn. Initial registry baseline assembled from cross-section sweep for items currently flagged pending user authorship or equivalent. This entry’s own title also flagged pending user authorship per slot 30 NO-NAMING recursively.
  tags: methodology, pending-user-authorship-registry, slot-30-NO-NAMING-anchor, t29-unnameability-anchor, citation-lock-anchor, may-2026-15-origin, working-title-recursive, registry-format, overhauled-2026-05-20, dual-audience-schema

Sense-3 = vocabulary (the polymyth-lexicon that names the operations the engine performs). Sense-4 = book series (the forthcoming Pentagram five-book series under the polymorphousmythology name). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B4. SABACHTAN. Three senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = ground (the framework-ground that all polymyth operations rest on; the gnosticism of what is already the case). Sense-2 = gnosticism (sabachtan gnosticism specifically as a religion-position contrasted with belief-religions; knowledge of what is already operating rather than assent to a proposition). Sense-3 = seminar gathering (Sabachtan Gnosticism Philosophy Seminar as a named event-form within the Seminar Schools institution). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

B5. JESTER. Two senses, T114h AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY ordering. Sense-1 = kayfabe-boundary action-mode (the structural-operation of holding the kayfabe/real distinction openly while operating inside the kayfabe; the licensed-fool-inside-the-court move that lets serious content land through comedy register). Sense-2 = Saul-Goodman analytic figure (the canonical analytic figure where the jester-mode is most-legibly worked out across Better Call Saul; the figure deployed at architecture-of-character scale rather than at operation-of-speech scale). Ratification or revision per stem at user discretion.

T106 PRIOR RECATEGORIZATION. MEPHISTODATA struck from Group B per T106 D1e directive (MEPHISTODATA is a set of characteristics, not multi-sense word-stem). Group B shrinks to five stems (CONCRESCENCE, CONFLUENCE, POLYMYTH, SABACHTAN, JESTER) per T114g audit.

T114h PRELIMINARY STATUS. User-directive T114g ’draft preliminary definitions and move on’ authorizes AI-PROPOSED-PRELIMINARY orderings without per-stem user-ratification gate. Each stem ordering above is operative until the user revises any of them. Per slot 30 amendment for the PUA section, AI-PROPOSED status is permitted inside this section.

GROUP C. METHODOLOGY ENTRY TITLE REVISIONS.

C1. Unnameability-of-cultural-form-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature (TTRPG canonical case, cross-tradition convergence-recognition, May 15 2026) at ml*.txt line 3565. T29 entry working title.

C2. "Site map (hidden link)" at ml*.txt SEED entry. T54 revision deprecated the parenthetical: the link is no longer hidden. Title rewrite needed.

C3. The title of this very registry entry. Recursive pending: the registry holding pending-titles needs its own title.

GROUP D. DRAFTED-NOT-COMMITTED METHODOLOGY ENTRIES.

D1. Sanderson library-of-mediums methodology entry (T28 draft). Entry-content drafted. Title pending and entry-itself pending user authorization to commit.

GROUP E. TTRPG-FAMILY NAMING DECISION.

E1. The umbrella term for the bb-cognate-system family. Per T29 unnameability principle, this may be ratifiable as unnameable-by-design rather than nameable. User authors either (a) a polymyth-side name (process-noun, gerund, or confluence-naming per T29's own suggestions), or (b) explicit ratification that the umbrella stays placeholder "TTRPG family" or "library-of-systems" indefinitely. Per T29: explicit ratification IS a valid framework move.

GROUP F. PENTAGRAM BOOK TITLES (CURRENTLY IN PENDING SECTION).

F1. Pentagram book 1 of 5. Title pending.

F2. Pentagram book 2 of 5. Title pending.

F3. Pentagram book 3 of 5. Title pending.

F4. Pentagram book 4 of 5. Working title exists: GORGONWARS: ARENDTIANFEMINISM, HEGELIANEGIRLS and FAUSTIANECHICKS ASSEMBLING THE HEGELIANEKITTY ASSEMBLAGE AGAINST THE GORGON HORDE. Ratification or revision pending.

F5. Pentagram book 5 of 5. Title pending.

GROUP G. FORTHCOMING CITATIONS FROM T29 ENTRY.

G1. Bergson. Time and Free Will. Citation [178] referenced as forthcoming in T29 entry. Citation entry pending author.

G2. Costikyan. Where Stories End and Games Begin. Game Developer Magazine 1994 with 2000 expansion. Citation entry pending author and number.

G3. Hindmarch. Second Person, MIT Press 2007, edited by Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin. Citation entry pending author and number.

G4. Sanderson. BYU English 318R writing-class lectures plus Q&A streams 2010 through 2026. Citation entry pending author and number.

G5. J.Z. Smith. Imagining Religion 1982. Citation entry pending author and number.

G6. Talal Asad. Genealogies of Religion 1993. Citation entry pending author and number.

G7. radiojamesgames. TTRPGs Need Genres, January 2026. Citation entry pending author and number.

T66 STATUS UPDATE (May 15 2026). Group G items moved from UNPROPOSED (per T58 deferral) to AI-PROPOSED. Seven citation entries committed to mc*.citation section with AI-PROPOSED canonical IDs awaiting user ratification of canonical numbers. G1 -> cit-044 (Bergson Time and Free Will 1889/1910 trans.). G2 -> cit-045 (Costikyan I Have No Words and I Must Design 1994/2002; caveat: user mentioned separate Where Stories End and Games Begin piece; specific title-and-venue could not be re-verified; user to confirm intended Costikyan source). G3 -> cit-046 (Second Person ed. Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin MIT Press 2007 ISBN 978-0-262-08356-0; caveat: specific Hindmarch essay-title within volume pending user identification). G4 -> cit-047 (Sanderson BYU English 318R lectures 2010-2026; caveat: lecture series spans years with recurrent treatment of play-to-medium-strengths principle; user to identify which specific lecture-year-and-segment carries the load if needed). G5 -> cit-048 (J.Z. Smith Imagining Religion Chicago 1982 ISBN 0-226-76358-4). G6 -> cit-049 (Talal Asad Genealogies of Religion Johns Hopkins 1993 ISBN 978-0-8018-4632-8). G7 -> cit-050 (radiojamesgames TTRPGs Need Genres January 2026; caveat: URL and full author byline pending user verification). The DEFERRED ITEMS clause for Group G is hereby superseded by AI-PROPOSED status; the citation-number assignment remains user authority and AI-PROPOSED numbers cit-044 through cit-050 await canonical ratification or renumbering.

GROUP H. STUDYLIST AND PENDING SECTION ITEMS AWAITING USER AUTHORSHIP.

H1. Wiener cross-tradition convergence-recognition methodology entry (currently studylist, mephistodata-pending). Substrate documented at citation [241] Wiener HUOHB chapter VII. Entry-name and canonical composition pending.

H2. Camels-ad bad-logos idiomary entry (canonical Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI violation example). Currently in PENDING section. Author and citation pending.

RULE AMENDMENT MAY 15 2026. User directive this turn amended slot 30 NO-NAMING. Original rule: AI does not coin canonical names; naming is user authorship; suggestions only on request. Amended rule: the original holds OUTSIDE this section. INSIDE this section (PENDING-USER-AUTHORSHIP), AI may coin proposed names marked AI-PROPOSED, awaiting user ratification. Three workflow states for each item: (1) UNPROPOSED (no AI-PROPOSED name yet; item awaits anyone's proposal). (2) AI-PROPOSED (AI has proposed a name; user may ratify-as-is, modify, or reject). (3) USER-RATIFIED (user has confirmed a canonical name; item moves out of this registry; entry in home section gets the canonical title; strike-through preserved here per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION). The amendment unblocks substantial framework work that had been stuck at the naming gate. Outside this section, slot 30 NO-NAMING continues in force. AI-coinage attempts outside this section remain canonical violations.

WHY THIS AMENDMENT IS STRUCTURALLY CONSISTENT. The original slot 30 rule treats naming as user authorship to prevent the AI from manufacturing framework-content from training-priors. Items in this section are explicitly catalogued as awaiting names; the user has already authored the category itself and has explicitly opened the exception. AI-PROPOSED names in this scope are not framework-coinage in the slot 30 sense; they are suggestions sitting in a sanctioned-suggestion-zone where user-ratification is the canonicalizing operation. The discipline preserved: AI does not unilaterally canonize names. The discipline relaxed: AI may propose without explicit per-item request, since the per-section directive serves as standing request.

AI-PROPOSED NAMES (May 15 2026 first pass, awaiting user ratification).

GROUP A. GATE ENTRIES. Working titles in current use ARE already the operational rule names; proposal is to ratify-as-canonical.

A1 AI-PROPOSED: "Citation-lock rule" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: the rule fires at the moment of citation; the name describes the operation directly; no shorter form carries the load better.

A2 AI-PROPOSED: "Generation-time gates list" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: distinguishes gates (rules that fire at generation time) from catalog (reference content); the architectural distinction this entry codifies needs the qualifier "generation-time" to land.

A3 AI-PROPOSED: "Multi-sense word stem registry" (ratify the working title). Reasoning: registry is the data-structure (lookup table); word-stem is the unit (root form); multi-sense is the qualifier that triggers the lookup; all three required for clarity.

A4 AI-PROPOSED: "Framework-loaded-versus-framework-running gap-question pattern" (shorter form of the working title; preserves the diagnostic substance). Reasoning: the original working title is descriptive but long; the gap-question pattern is what the strategy operationally consists of; this form is searchable and citation-friendly. Alternative: keep the longer form. User picks.

GROUP C. METHODOLOGY ENTRY TITLE REVISIONS.

C1 AI-PROPOSED: "Unnameability as natural degorgonifying signature (TTRPG canonical case)". Reasoning: shorter than the working title; preserves the structural claim plus the canonical case reference; drops the date and the cross-tradition-convergence qualifier from the title (those move to the entry's metadata or body).

C2 AI-PROPOSED: "Sitemap navigational hub (polymyth namespace)". Reasoning: the "hidden link" parenthetical is structurally false post-T54 move; "navigational hub" describes the function; "polymyth namespace" locates it.

C3 AI-PROPOSED: "Pending-user-authorship registry" (ratify the working title without the parenthetical). Reasoning: the working title has a long parenthetical explaining the recursion; the parenthetical can move to the entry body; the core noun-phrase is sufficient.

GROUP D. SANDERSON METHODOLOGY ENTRY (DRAFTED NOT COMMITTED).

D1 AI-PROPOSED: "Library-of-mediums rule (Sanderson play-to-medium-strengths applied to AI-delivered story-game content)". Reasoning: parallel-construction with bb* library-of-systems and chassis-of-chassis; Sanderson attribution preserved in the parenthetical for the citation lineage; rule operationally states what it does. Pending the entry itself being committed.

GROUP E. TTRPG-FAMILY NAMING DECISION.

E1 AI-PROPOSED: ratify unnameable-by-design per T29. Reasoning: T29 entry already establishes the diagnostic criteria for unnameable-by-design forms; TTRPG case satisfies all six conditions; explicit ratification is a valid framework move per T29. Continue using "TTRPG family" or "library-of-systems" as placeholders without committing to canonical umbrella. Alternative: user authors a process-noun coinage instead. User picks.

GROUP H. STUDYLIST AND PENDING SECTION ITEMS.

H1 AI-PROPOSED: "Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)". Reasoning: identifies the convergence-substrate (Wiener's apprentice-sorcerer indictment of administrators-of-classification); names the cross-tradition relationship to the framework's existing naming-discipline; preserves Wiener's tradition-anchor without capture.

H2 AI-PROPOSED: "Camels-ad bad-logos canonical violation (Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI worked example)". Reasoning: names the operational status (canonical violation example); references the rule it violates; positions it as the idiomary worked-example.

DEFERRED ITEMS (AI declines to propose despite category permission).

GROUP B. MULTI-SENSE WORD STEM SENSE-NUMBERING. The senses themselves were AI-drafted; the canonical numbering is user-authored because sense-numbering encodes which sense is foundational versus derivative. That ranking is a user judgment call about the framework's conceptual architecture. AI defers.

GROUP F. PENTAGRAM BOOK TITLES (5 items). Book titles are creative authorship in a different register than methodology naming. The category permission technically allows AI proposals here, but the work-substrate is the user's authorship throughout. AI defers; proposing book titles for someone else's books would substitute AI generation for the user's creative process. User authors directly.

GROUP G. FORTHCOMING CITATIONS (7 items). [SUPERSEDED T66] Originally listed in DEFERRED ITEMS pending user direction. User T66 directive ("do anymore you can on your own") served as standing request per the original clause's invitation. AI-PROPOSED canonical IDs cit-044 through cit-050 committed to mc*.citation section T66 with full bibliographic data (author + work + year + ISBN where available) and caveats noted for uncertain claims. Citation numbers remain AI-PROPOSED pending user ratification or renumbering. See T66 STATUS UPDATE block above for per-item ID assignments and caveats. Per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION, the original deferral clause is preserved here as historical record.

RATIFICATIONS MAY 15 2026 T58 (per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION; items preserved).

User ratified all 12 AI-PROPOSED names from T57 first pass. Each item below records the ratification. Items now have canonical titles in their home sections; their working-title flags have been removed. Items remain in this registry as historical record per Rule 6.

A1 RATIFIED: "Citation-lock rule" (canonical title in methodology section).
A2 RATIFIED: "Generation-time gates list" (canonical title in coreplus section).
A3 RATIFIED: "Multi-sense word stem registry" (canonical title in coreplus section).
A4 RATIFIED: "Framework-loaded-versus-framework-running gap-question pattern (Category B unconscious-user-degorgonification-strategy, May 15 2026)" (canonical title in learnings section).
C1 RATIFIED: "Unnameability as natural degorgonifying signature (TTRPG canonical case)" (canonical title in methodology section).
C2 RATIFIED: "Sitemap navigational hub (polymyth namespace)" (canonical title in methodology section, replacing the stale "Site map (hidden link)" working title).
C3 RATIFIED: "Pending-user-authorship registry" (canonical title for THIS very entry; the recursive pending-on-itself state resolved).
D1 RATIFIED (NAME RESERVED): "Library-of-mediums rule (Sanderson play-to-medium-strengths applied to AI-delivered story-game content)" approved as canonical title. Entry itself awaits separate user authorization to commit to ml*.
E1 RATIFIED (STRUCTURAL): TTRPG-family umbrella affirmed unnameable-by-design per T29 unnameability principle. Placeholder labels "TTRPG family" and "library-of-systems" stay non-canonical; the framework declines to coin a canonical umbrella term per its own unnameability methodology.
H1 RATIFIED: "Wiener apprentice-sorcerer convergence (HUOHB chapter VII parallel to slot 30 naming-discipline)" (canonical title in studylist section).
H2 RATIFIED: "Camels-ad bad-logos canonical violation (Rule 1 CITES+LOGOI worked example)" (canonical title in pending section).

ITEMS STILL AWAITING USER AUTHORSHIP. Group B (FIVE multi-sense word-stem sense-numberings remaining after T106 MEPHISTODATA recategorization); Group F (five Pentagram book titles, user creative authorship retained); Group G [CLOSED T106]. Remaining items are Group B (5) + Group F (5) = 10 items as of T106.

T106 RATIFICATIONS 2026-05-21 (per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION). User T106 directive ('i dont care i just need all the citations later'): canonical citation numbers cit-044 through cit-050 RATIFIED at AI-proposed values from T66. The AI-PROPOSED status flag drops; canonical numbers stand.

G1 cit-044 RATIFIED. Bergson, Time and Free Will (1889, 1910 translation).
G2 cit-045 RATIFIED. Costikyan, I Have No Words and I Must Design (1994, 2002 expansion). Caveat: separate Where Stories End and Games Begin piece could not be re-verified; user confirms intended source at use-time.
G3 cit-046 RATIFIED. Second Person edited by Harrigan and Wardrip-Fruin (MIT Press 2007, ISBN 978-0-262-08356-0). Caveat: specific Hindmarch essay-title within volume pending user identification at use-time.
G4 cit-047 RATIFIED. Sanderson, BYU English 318R lectures spanning 2010 to 2026. Caveat: which specific lecture-year-and-segment carries the load identifiable at use-time.
G5 cit-048 RATIFIED. J.Z. Smith, Imagining Religion (Chicago 1982, ISBN 0-226-76358-4).
G6 cit-049 RATIFIED. Talal Asad, Genealogies of Religion (Johns Hopkins 1993, ISBN 978-0-8018-4632-8).
G7 cit-050 RATIFIED. radiojamesgames, TTRPGs Need Genres (January 2026). Caveat: URL and full author byline pending verification at use-time.

T106 RECATEGORIZATION. Group B5 MEPHISTODATA struck from Group B per user D1e directive ('this is a set of characteristics'). MEPHISTODATA characteristics now live in the methodologylist entry at META RULE STATUS section (relabeled three-senses to three-characteristics same turn). Group B shrinks to five word-stem registry items.

REGISTRY SHRINKAGE METRIC. Pre-T57: 24+ items pending across the framework. Post-T58: 18 items remaining (6 + 5 + 7) plus future additions. Twelve items moved from UNPROPOSED through AI-PROPOSED to USER-RATIFIED in a single workflow round, validating the three-state mechanism.

PROTOCOL FOR REMOVAL FROM THIS REGISTRY. When user authors a name for any item here, the entry for that item is struck through (preserved per Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION), the entry in its home section has its title updated, and a one-line entry in this registry's own provenance section records the move. The registry shrinks as user-authorship lands.

CROSS-REFERENCES. Unnameability-of-cultural-form-as-natural-degorgonifying-signature methodology entry at ml*.txt line 3565 (T29 canonical case justifying this registry's existence; verified-against-ml*-this-turn per Citation-lock rule, scope: cultural-form unnameability principle applied here at the framework-internal naming-discipline layer). Slot 30 NO-NAMING + NO-FABRICATION methodology entry (the parent discipline). Citation-lock rule methodology entry (the gate that catches pending-authorship items in framework citations). PENDING section (adjacent category for user-decision-blocked items; structurally distinct). STUDYLIST section (adjacent category for substrate-development items; structurally distinct). Rule 6 CONSOLIDATION NOT DELETION (governs removal protocol).

May 15 2026 conversation T56. User directive: "’pending user authorship’ its own category, and have the justification be our conversation about the difficulty of naming ttrpg or wtvr." Section authored same turn. Section name (pending-user-authorship) authored by the user this turn. Initial registry baseline assembled from cross-section sweep for items currently flagged pending user authorship or equivalent. This entry’s own title also flagged pending user authorship per slot 30 NO-NAMING recursively.
  tags: methodology, pending-user-authorship-registry, slot-30-NO-NAMING-anchor, t29-unnameability-anchor, citation-lock-anchor, may-2026-15-origin, working-title-recursive, registry-format, overhauled-2026-05-20, dual-audience-schema

title: RESOLVED NAME SLOT: fourth table failure named WRITTEN-FOR-THE-INITIATED (bb* dm-062, CL-35)
body:
[both] RESOLVED NAME SLOT: fourth table failure named WRITTEN-FOR-THE-INITIATED (bb* dm-062, CL-35)
The fourth same-day table failure (bb* dm-062) carries a descriptive title and awaits its name. AI-PROPOSED candidates, marked as such per standing rule: PASSWORD-PROSE; WRITTEN-FOR-THE-INITIATED. Rainbowsol stamps one, replaces both, or leaves the slot standing.

RESOLVED 2026-06-06. Rainbowsol delegated the naming, his words, this does not matter, you can name it yourself. The AI stamped WRITTEN-FOR-THE-INITIATED, the sibling form of WRITTEN-FOR-THE-AUTHOR. PASSWORD-PROSE is retired. Recorded at bb* dm-062 the same pass.

Opened 2026-06-06 with the CL batch. Naming is user authorship; AI proposals live only in this section.
  tags: pending-user-authorship, ai-proposed, name-slot, fourth-failure, cl-35, 2026-06-06
